top_news_headlines.headline: Brent crude holds above $105/bbl as Iran seizes two ships in Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire extension
Trump extends US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely but cancels Kushner/Witkoff Islamabad peace talks trip
S&P 500 and Nasdaq close at records on April 24; Intel surges 23% on strong earnings, semiconductors rally 18th straight day
India's Nifty IT index falls ~10% in one week — steepest decline since March 2020 — as Infosys and HCLTech cut guidance
FIIs return as net buyers on April 24 for first time in 7 weeks; India receives $106 mn inflow after cumulative $5 bn outflow
JP Morgan downgrades Indian equities to 'Neutral' from 'Overweight' citing high valuations and energy shock earnings headwinds
Bank of England deputy governor warns global stock markets are overvalued and due for correction amid geopolitical risks
GIFT Nifty trading at 24,134 (+0.75%) indicating a positive opening for Indian markets next week if geopolitical situation stabilises

iran_hormuz_status.latest_development: Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely (as of April 25) but cancelled envoys Kushner and Witkoff's planned Islamabad peace talks trip. Iran's Revolutionary Guard seized two international (non-US, non-Israeli) vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. White House stated this does not violate the ceasefire. Iran's parliamentary speaker called the US naval blockade a ceasefire violation. US blockade of the Strait continues.
iran_hormuz_status.crude_impact: Brent crude at $105.33/bbl, up ~17% week-on-week. Continued Hormuz uncertainty sustaining oil above $100. Weighing heavily on India's current account deficit, rupee (USD/INR ~94.26), and inflation outlook.
